level_signals: 8+ years of experience in a product or other technical role
High degree of autonomy and drive
Expected to help define consumer strategy working directly with Link leadership
Set the pace, prioritization and execution for the team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Brings 8+ years of experience in a product or other technical role

Has experience working on world-class consumer products at scale. Has a high-level of taste, judgment and design empathy. Is able to use data and insights rigorously to analyze the performance of products.
Brings a desire for continuous product improvement and can optimize for shipping a portfolio of small, medium, and large releases.
Has managed high-stakes external partnerships, e.g. agreeing on milestones and executing on an integration into your product.
Has strong written and verbal communication skills with a talent for precise articulations of customer problems.
Takes an ownership mindset and works on whatever it takes to solve problems and delight consumers.
Can put themselves in the shoes of consumers and be a steward of crafting great experiences.
Can dig deep into the data, think from first principles, and deliver the right results. Can write and use SQL as needed.
Has the ability to thrive with a high level of autonomy and responsibility.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working on consumer financial products.
Conducting product market research and building buy-in for product ideas from internal stakeholders and leadership.
Experience building brand new products or working in a startup environment.
Experience working with GTM teams to bring a new product to market.

scope.surface: Link App experience on web and native — wallet product for consumers including payments, identity, and emerging modalities
scope.business_model: B2C consumer wallet product within a B2B payments infrastructure platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Consumer adoption and engagement with Link (200M+ users, growing rapidly)
Product release cadence (small, medium, large releases)
core_responsibilities: Build the Link App experience on web and native
Deeply understand user needs by spending time with consumers and cross-functional teams
Be the voice of the consumers within Link and at Stripe
Help set an ambitious roadmap for product development and expansion of Link's capabilities
Discover new parts of the business and lead them from nascent stages to maturity
Set the pace, prioritization and execution for the team
Collaborate with stakeholders across Engineering, Design, Data Science, Marketing, Sales, Account Management, and Operations

green_flags: Large and fast-growing consumer surface (200M+ users)
Ambitious and forward-looking product vision (AI agents, stablecoins, identity)
Encouraged to apply if meeting minimum requirements — signals inclusivity
Clear distinction between minimum requirements and preferred qualifications
Cross-functional ownership and direct access to Link leadership
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated — candidates cannot assess logistics
SQL listed as a hard requirement ('as needed') which may screen out pure consumer PMs without data skills
JD trailing bullet under Preferred qualifications is empty — possible formatting/copy error

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and leadership/strategy signals)
location.type (not stated in JD; marked as unclear)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and Stripe's platform context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no mention of equity in JD)
ambiguities: Location and remote/hybrid policy are entirely unstated — could be onsite, hybrid, or remote; marked as unclear

The JD describes the PM as 'technical' but the minimum requirements do not require an engineering background — archetype confidence is high but the technical bar is softer than a strict Technical PM role
The trailing empty bullet under Preferred qualifications is likely a formatting artifact and has been ignored
